tests.intent: One session-bound relational R2 pixel macro exposes the reviewed six-column dense frame contract without persisting renderer output.
All seven reviewed scene frames contain exactly 64,000 rows and exactly one row for every coordinate pair.
Every column zero through 319 owns every row zero through 199 with no gaps, duplicates, or out-of-range coordinates.
Every canonical scene pixel is a non-null integer palette index in the inclusive zero through 255 range.
A raised far floor clips the portal, draws the directed lower piece, and assigns reverse-projected floor samples to the correct interval sector.
A portal with both floor and ceiling differences preserves its open center while drawing independently projected lower and upper pieces.
Adjacent sky ceilings suppress their upper wall piece and sample full-bright SKY1 without applying the sector COLORMAP band.
Flat and wall animation frames are selected from checked-in groups using persisted current tic and deterministic group periods.
Wall horizontal sampling combines analytic wall coordinate, seg offset, and signed sidedef x offset while vertical sampling includes signed y offset.
The upper-unpegged flag aligns upper texture origin to the opposite ceiling plus texture height before applying sidedef y offset.
The lower-unpegged flag aligns lower texture origin to the facing ceiling before applying signed sidedef y offset.
Seven complete 64,000-byte frames have exact independent SHA-256 values and repeat without token or row-source-order dependence.
Repeated evaluation for one unchanged session yields exactly the same 64,000 coordinate rows and canonical palette-byte hash.
Floor and ceiling reverse projection selects the sector interval containing each reconstructed distance rather than the nearest hit sector globally.
Sky sampling ignores sector light changes while retaining deterministic horizontal camera mapping and vertical texture coordinates.
Animation advances by integer tic period, wraps each declared group, and never substitutes an asset from another group.
Signed offsets and negative texture coordinates use floor modulus for arbitrary asset dimensions rather than Oracle MOD.
Upper and lower roles use distinct flag bits and height anchors, including signed y offset after role-specific origin selection.
Non-sky wall, floor, and ceiling texels use active-sector light and the floored clamped COLORMAP band exactly once.
Set-based source, anti-cheat restrictions, and all eighteen isolated semantic mutations are independently checked and fail closed.
